---
title: "ECON 3209 — Econometrics with Python"
page-layout: full
title-block-banner: false
---

::: {.hero-banner}
# ECON 3209 — Econometrics with Python

**BSc Hons Cooperation & Banking | 6th Semester**  
Kerala Agricultural University · 2025–26

*A 20-week hands-on journey from Python fundamentals to applied econometrics — with every lecture interactive in your browser.*
:::

## Course Overview

This course equips students with the quantitative and computational skills needed to analyse economic and agricultural data. Using **Python** throughout, you will progress from programming basics to implementing the full suite of classical econometric models — all within an interactive environment where code runs live in your browser (no installation required).

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="48%"}
### What You Will Learn

- Python programming for data analysis
- Data wrangling and visualisation with Pandas & Matplotlib
- Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) estimation and inference
- Diagnosing and correcting regression problems
- Panel data, qualitative response, and time series models
:::
::: {.column width="4%"}
:::
::: {.column width="48%"}
### Course Details

| | |
|---|---|
| **Course Code** | ECON 3209 |
| **Credits** | 3 |
| **Semester** | 6th |
| **Programme** | BSc Hons Cooperation & Banking |
| **University** | Kerala Agricultural University |
| **Duration** | 20 Weeks × 3 Lectures |
| **Total Lectures** | 60 |
:::
:::

---

## Course Structure

### 🐍 Phase 1 — Python Foundations (Weeks 1–6)

::: {.week-card}
**Week 1** · [Python Environment & Basics](slides/week-01/index.qmd)

Getting started with Python · Variables & data types · Operators & expressions
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 2** · [Control Flow & Functions](slides/week-02/index.qmd)

Conditionals & loops · Functions & scope · List comprehensions & lambda
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 3** · [NumPy for Economics](slides/week-03/index.qmd)

Arrays & vectorisation · Linear algebra · Statistical operations
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 4** · [Pandas & Data Wrangling](slides/week-04/index.qmd)

Series & DataFrames · Merge / reshape / groupby · Real agricultural data
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 5** · [Data Visualisation](slides/week-05/index.qmd)

Matplotlib foundations · Seaborn statistical charts · Interactive plots
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 6** · [Working with Real Data](slides/week-06/index.qmd)

Import / export & APIs · Exploratory data analysis · Data cleaning case study
:::

---

### 📊 Phase 2 — Econometrics (Weeks 7–20)

::: {.week-card}
**Week 7** · [Introduction to Econometrics](slides/week-07/index.qmd)

What is econometrics? · Economic data types · Simple OLS derivation
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 8** · [OLS: Theory & Properties](slides/week-08/index.qmd)

Gauss-Markov theorem · Statistical properties · Python OLS from scratch
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 9** · [Inference in OLS](slides/week-09/index.qmd)

Hypothesis testing · Confidence intervals · *t* and *F* tests in Python
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 10** · [Multiple Linear Regression](slides/week-10/index.qmd)

MLR setup · Interpretation · Partial effects & Python implementation
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 11** · [Multicollinearity](slides/week-11/index.qmd)

Detection · Consequences · Remedies & VIF in Python
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 12** · [Heteroscedasticity](slides/week-12/index.qmd)

Nature & consequences · Detection tests · WLS & robust standard errors
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 13** · [Dummy Variables](slides/week-13/index.qmd)

Qualitative predictors · Interaction terms · Structural breaks in Python
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 14** · [Nonlinear Regression](slides/week-14/index.qmd)

Log & polynomial models · Splines · Python curve fitting
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 15** · [Qualitative Response Models](slides/week-15/index.qmd)

Linear probability model · Logit · Probit in Python
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 16** · [Model Specification](slides/week-16/index.qmd)

Omitted variable bias · RESET test · AIC / BIC model selection
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 17** · [Panel Data Analysis](slides/week-17/index.qmd)

Pooled vs fixed vs random effects · Hausman test · Python implementation
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 18** · [Autocorrelation](slides/week-18/index.qmd)

Nature & causes · Durbin-Watson & Breusch-Godfrey tests · Cochrane-Orcutt
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 19** · [Time Series: Basics](slides/week-19/index.qmd)

Stationarity & unit roots · ACF / PACF · ADF test in Python
:::

::: {.week-card}
**Week 20** · [Time Series: Forecasting](slides/week-20/index.qmd)

ARIMA models · Forecast evaluation · Course review
:::

---

> **Interactive Slides:** All lecture slides include live Python code cells powered by [Pyodide](https://pyodide.org) — run Python directly in your browser, no installation needed.
